base_html:
Home  /  Anti-Ragging

Anti-Ragging Policy

Zero Tolerance · Safe Campus · Strict Action · Confidential Reporting

Zero Tolerance Policy

Ragging is a Punishable Offense

UGIP maintains a complete zero-tolerance policy towards ragging in any form. Strict disciplinary action including suspension, expulsion, and legal proceedings will be taken against offenders. The institute strictly follows AICTE, UBTER, and Government of India guidelines.

🚫
Strict Disciplinary Action

Suspension, expulsion from institute, and fines. Legal complaint filed with police if required.

🛡️
Safe & Inclusive Campus

We promote dignity, equality, and mutual respect among all students and staff members.

📣
Confidential Reporting

Students can report incidents anonymously and without any fear of retaliation whatsoever.

📋
AICTE Compliance

Fully compliant with AICTE Regulations 2009 and UGC Regulations on Curbing Ragging 2009.

Anti-Ragging Committee 2025–26

Committee constituted as per AICTE/UBTER norms to prevent and address ragging incidents proactively across the campus.

What Constitutes Ragging?

As per UGC/AICTE regulations, ragging includes any act which causes physical or psychological harm to any student:

  • Teasing, embarrassing, or humiliating any student.
  • Forcing a student to do any act that causes shame or discomfort.
  • Verbal abuse, threats, or intimidation of any kind.
  • Any form of physical assault or harassment.
  • Psychological harm, mental torture, or academic disruption.

Punishment for Ragging

Punishment varies based on the nature and severity of the incident, and may include:

  • Immediate cancellation of admission.
  • Suspension from attending classes and academic privileges.
  • Withholding results, scholarships, and certificates.
  • Debarring from examinations and campus placements.
  • Filing of an FIR and initiating criminal proceedings with local police.
📞

Anti-Ragging Helpline

If you witness or experience ragging, report it immediately. Your identity will be strictly protected and kept confidential.

1800-180-5522
UGC Anti-Ragging Helpline (Toll Free, 24x7)
File Online Complaint via antiragging.in
